sound_type: vertical rise + crash -- Southern Resident Killer Whale rises vertically above surface, holds 2-10 seconds (observation phase), descends. E=8 water displacement event.
ecosystem: Inland sea -- Salish Sea, San Juan Islands, Puget Sound, Strait of Juan de Fuca
season: Resident (year-round Southern Residents in Salish Sea)
milestone: Orca arc degree 4 of 7 (degrees 270-276). Cultural behavior documented: spy-hop is a learned, culturally transmitted behavior. The orca rising to observe the above-water world. E=8 following E=9 apex at degree 273.

coordinates.description: Southern Resident range: Salish Sea, San Juan Islands, Strait of Juan de Fuca. Spy-hopping observed throughout range, particularly in complex surface environments (vessel traffic, prey activity).

behavioral_context.spy_hop_description: Controlled vertical rise until head (and pectoral fins in full spy-hop) clears the water surface. Hold 2-10 seconds in observation phase. Directional orientation toward point of interest. Controlled descent return. Not a breach -- the animal does not leave the water entirely and does not crash laterally.
behavioral_context.function: Primarily observational: gathering visual information about the above-water environment. Documented directional orientation toward vessels, other animals, shoreline features. Cultural transmission: learned by young orcas observing adults.
behavioral_context.cultural_transmission: Meets all three criteria for cultural transmission: within-population variation, learned through observation during development, persists through teaching not genetics. One of the clearest examples of cultural behavior in non-human animals.
behavioral_context.visual_capability: Bifocal vision: orca eyes adapted for function in both water and air. Spy-hop eliminates Snell's window restriction, providing unrestricted aerial view of immediate environment.

sound_metrics.amplitude: E=8. Controlled descent water impact. Substantial but not the E=9 of a lateral breach body-slam. Water displacement on ascent; near-silence during hold; descent water return at E=8.
sound_metrics.key_property: E=8 following E=9 apex (breach, degree 273). Vertical rise + hold + descent. Culturally transmitted observational behavior. Directional orientation toward points of interest. Hold duration 2-10 seconds above surface.

nodes.title: Cultural Transmission -- Learned Behavior
nodes.description: Spy-hop is culturally transmitted: within-population variation, learned during development, persists through teaching. Young orcas learn from adults. One of the clearest examples of cultural behavior in non-human animals.

nodes.title: Cognitive Model of Above-Water World
nodes.description: To spy-hop deliberately and orient toward a point of interest requires a cognitive model extending beyond primary underwater sensory environment. The orca has a concept of the above-water world as information source.

nodes.title: Bifocal Vision -- Snell's Window Eliminated
nodes.description: Orca eyes adapted for both water and air vision. Spy-hop eliminates Snell's window restriction -- unrestricted aerial view of immediate environment. Information gain justifies energy expenditure.

nodes.title: Breach vs. Spy-Hop -- Force vs. Observation
nodes.description: Two consecutive above-water behaviors: breach (273, E=9, maximum force, function uncertain) vs. spy-hop (274, E=8, controlled, observational, function clearer). One goes completely; the other watches carefully.

nodes.title: Orca Arc Degree 4/7
nodes.description: Degrees 270-276. 273 was energy maximum (breach E=9). 274 is observational behavior (spy-hop E=8). Arc documents behavioral repertoire: vocal (270), species (271), predation (272), force (273), observation (274), percussion (275), echolocation (276).

cross_references.relationship: Preceding entry. Breach (maximum force, E=9) vs. spy-hop (observation, E=8). Two above-water behaviors in consecutive degrees. Arc descends one step from energy apex.
SRKW primary prey. Spy-hopping may assist surface prey detection -- surface-schooling Chinook visible from above water. Salmon-orca dependency shapes all SRKW behavioral ecology.
Next entry. Spy-hop (vertical observation) to pec-slap (horizontal percussion). Observation to rhythmic social behavior.
E=8 combined E=16. Cultural curiosity and political engagement. The orca rises to observe; Vexed engage with theirs.
